title: The Pottery Class
introKo:
도자기 공방 첫 수업, Clay 선생님이 물레를 손수 가르쳐 주는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.

quiz.q: Clay 선생님이 물레에서 가장 먼저 가르쳐 준 것은?

normal.text: Your first pottery class begins tonight. The studio is warm and smells of wet earth. Mrs. Clay welcomes you at the door.
"Tonight we work with our hands," she says. "Clay does not listen to words. It only listens to fingers."
She puts a ball of wet clay on the wheel. "Do not be afraid to get your hands wet," she says. "Clay loves warm hands."

options.en: I have never touched clay before.
options.reaction: "Then tonight will be special," she smiles. "Untouched clay learns the fastest."

options.en: My hands are ready. Where do we start?
options.reaction: "Right here," she says. "The wheel loves ready hands."

normal.text: The wheel begins to turn. The wet clay slips and slides under your fingers. It will not stay still.
"First, you must find the center," Mrs. Clay says. She steps behind you and puts her hands over yours. "Feel the rhythm. Do not fight it."

options.en: It keeps slipping. Maybe you should steady my hands.
options.ko: 자꾸 미끄러지네요. 선생님이 제 손 좀 잡아 주셔야겠어요.
options.reaction: "Then hold it firmer, dear," she says softly. "Good hands are firm and gentle at the same time."
"Exactly," she says. "Slow and steady. You learn fast."

normal.text: Together, four hands press the clay, and it finds its center. "There," she says, stepping back. "When it is centered, it stops slipping and starts to dance."
"Now we pull it up. Wet your hands again — clay only rises when it is wet." Slowly, the clay rises between your fingers.
"Gently now," she says. "Squeeze softly and let it rise. See how tall it stands? It only rises like this for patient hands."

options.en: I did not know my hands could do this.
options.ko: 제 손이 이런 걸 할 수 있는 줄 몰랐어요.
options.reaction: "Every hand can," she says. "It only needs the right teacher. Lucky for you, I give private lessons."

options.en: Is it always this warm in here?
options.reaction: "That is the kiln, dear," she laughs. "Things get much hotter before they are finished."

normal.text: At the end of class, your little bowl goes into the kiln. "Now the fire finishes what our hands began," Mrs. Clay says. "The heat makes it strong."
You wait beside the kiln, and its warm light glows on both your faces. "Good things are never rushed," Mrs. Clay says softly. "The longer they take the heat, the stronger they become."

options.en: I will wait for it to cool. Good things take time.
options.ko: 식을 때까지 기다릴게요. 좋은 건 시간이 걸리니까요.
options.reaction: "Patient hands, again," she nods. "You already understand the part that matters most."

options.en: Then maybe I should come back every night.
options.ko: 그럼 저 매일 밤 다시 와야겠는데요.
options.reaction: "Every night?" she smiles. "Careful — I might start leaving the door open just for you."

normal.text: You walk home in the cool night air. Your hands still remember the rhythm of the wheel. Next week, you will come back — for the pottery, of course.

hard.text: Your first pottery class begins tonight, in a studio that smells of wet earth and warm dust. Mrs. Clay greets you at the door with an easy smile.
"Tonight we let our hands do the talking," she announces. "Clay ignores words. It answers only to fingers."
She drops a ball of wet clay onto the wheel. "Don't be afraid of getting your hands wet," she says. "Clay has always preferred warm hands."

options.en: I have never touched clay before in my life.
options.ko: 저 점토 태어나서 처음 만져 봐요.
options.reaction: "Then tonight will be a special one," she smiles. "Untouched clay learns the fastest."

options.en: My hands are ready. Where do we start?
options.reaction: "Right here," she says. "The wheel has a weakness for ready hands."

hard.text: The wheel begins to spin, and the wet clay slips and slides under your fingers, refusing to stay where you want it.
"Before anything else, you must find the center," Mrs. Clay says, stepping behind you and folding her hands over yours. "Feel the rhythm of the wheel. Don't fight it — move with it."

options.en: It keeps slipping away. Perhaps you should steady my hands yourself.
options.ko: 자꾸 미끄러져 빠져나가네요. 선생님이 직접 제 손을 잡아 주셔야 할 것 같은데요.
options.reaction: "Then hold it a little firmer, dear," she murmurs. "The best hands are firm and gentle at the very same time."
"Exactly so," she says. "Slow and steady. You learn remarkably fast."

hard.text: Together, four hands press down, and the clay finally finds its center. "There," she says, stepping back. "Once it is centered, it stops slipping away and starts to dance."
"Now comes the pull. Wet your hands again — clay only rises when it is properly wet." Slowly, almost shyly, the clay rises between your fingers.
"Gently now," she says. "A soft squeeze, a patient lift, and let it rise. See how tall it stands? It rises like this only for hands that take their time."

options.en: I never knew my hands could do this.
options.ko: 제 손이 이런 걸 할 수 있는 줄 몰랐어요.
options.reaction: "Every hand can," she says. "It simply needs the right teacher. Lucky for you, I give private lessons."

options.en: Is it always this warm in here?
options.reaction: "That would be the kiln, dear," she laughs. "Things get considerably hotter before they are finished."

hard.text: At the end of class, your little bowl disappears into the kiln. "Now the fire finishes what our hands began," Mrs. Clay says. "Nothing becomes strong without a little heat."
You linger beside the kiln, its warm light glowing on both of your faces. "Good things should never be rushed," Mrs. Clay murmurs. "The longer they hold the heat, the stronger they become."

options.en: I will wait for it to cool. The good things always take time.
options.ko: 식을 때까지 기다릴게요. 좋은 건 원래 시간이 걸리는 법이니까요.
options.reaction: "Patient hands once more," she nods. "You already understand the part that matters most of all."

options.en: Then perhaps I should come back every single night.
options.ko: 그럼 저 매일 밤이라도 다시 와야겠는데요.
options.reaction: "Every night?" she smiles. "Careful — I may start leaving the door open just for you."

hard.text: You walk home through the cool night air, your hands still remembering the rhythm of the wheel. Next week you will be back — for the pottery, naturally.

studio.en: a room where art is made

clay.en: soft wet earth for making pots

work.en: to use effort to do something

fingers.en: the long parts of your hand

wheel.en: a spinning table for shaping clay

spin.en: to turn around and around fast

firmer.ko: 더 단단히, 더 힘 있게 (firm의 비교급)

steady.ko: 꾸준한, 흔들림 없는 (동사로는 '흔들리지 않게 잡아 주다')

pull.en: to draw something up or toward you

between.en: in the space from one to another

know.en: to have something in your mind

teacher.en: a person who helps you learn

kiln.en: a very hot oven for baking pottery

announces.en: says loudly for everyone to hear

onto.en: to a place on top of

weakness.ko: 약점 (have a weakness for: ~에 약하다, ~을 아주 좋아하다)
weakness.en: something you cannot say no to

blamed.en: said something was the cause (past of blame)

claimed.en: took as your own (past of claim)

cradle.en: to hold gently in your hands or arms

glows.en: shines with a soft, warm light

glowing.en: shining with a soft, warm light

guide.en: to lead or show the way

instinct.en: a natural feeling for what to do

leaving.en: letting something stay a certain way

light.en: brightness; the shine of a lamp or fire

line.en: a long thin mark or shape

matters.ko: 중요하다 (the part that matters: 중요한 부분)

met.en: came face to face with (past of meet)

nods.en: moves the head down to agree

offer.en: to say you will give or do something

picture.ko: 그림 같은 전형 (the picture of: ~의 표본)

solid.ko: 꽉 찬 (booked solid: 예약이 꽉 찬)

summer.en: the hot season of the year

teases.en: jokes with someone in a playful way

announced.en: said in a clear, strong way (past of announce)

beginner.en: a person who is just starting

ends.en: the last parts; the two far points

found.en: thought it was; discovered (past of find)

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

kept.en: made continue; did not stop (past of keep)

key.en: a small metal thing that opens a lock

leave.en: to go away; to let stay

loud.en: out loud means spoken so others hear

named.en: said the name of (past of name)

noticed.en: saw and paid attention (past of notice)

ones.ko: 사람들, 것들 (the quiet ones: 조용한 사람들)
ones.en: people or things of a certain kind

play.en: to act a certain way; play it safe means take no risks

promise.en: words you say you will keep; to say you surely will

promising.en: saying you surely will do something

smiled.en: made a happy face (past of smile)

something.en: a thing you do not name

visit.en: to go to see a person or place

wink.clay: 재료 이름이자 선생님 성함이에요. 'Clay는 따뜻한 손을 좋아해요' 같은 문장들, 재료 얘기로 한 번, 자기소개로 한 번 — 두 번 읽어 보세요.
wink.wet: 점토는 젖어 있어야 빚어져요. …이 말을 공방 밖에서 하면 분위기가 조금 달라질 수 있어요.
wink.rhythm: 물레의 리듬을 느끼라는 조언이지만, 둘이 호흡을 맞추는 장면에서는 묘하게 들리죠.
wink.firmer: '조금 더 단단히'는 순수한 물레 조언이에요. 등 뒤에서 나직이 말하면 얘기가 달라지지만요.
wink.rises: 점토가 손끝에서 천천히 올라오는 것뿐인데, 왠지 문장을 두 번 읽게 돼요.
wink.squeeze: 부드럽게 쥐라는 정식 도예 용어예요. 맥락이 맥락이라 귀가 먼저 반응할 뿐이에요.
wink.pull: '끌어올리기'는 실제 물레 기법 이름이에요. 최대한 진지한 표정으로 읽어 주세요.
wink.hotter: 가마는 원래 갈수록 뜨거워져요. 이 대화도 그런 것 같지만 기분 탓이에요.
wink.heat: 열기가 있어야 단단해진다는 건 어디까지나 도자기 얘기예요. 아마도요.
wink.door: 공방 문을 열어 둔다는 건 늦게까지 반겨 준다는 뜻이에요. 그 이상은 각자의 상상에 맡길게요.

winkEn.clay: It's the material — and it's her name. 'Clay loves warm hands' works as a pottery fact and as an introduction. Read it both ways.
winkEn.wet: Clay has to stay wet to be shaped. Say that line outside the studio and watch the room change temperature.
winkEn.rhythm: Perfectly sound wheel advice: find the rhythm. It just lands differently when two people are keeping it together.
winkEn.firmer: 'A little firmer' is honest pottery coaching. Murmured from just behind your shoulder, it starts to sound like something else.
winkEn.rises: The clay is simply rising between your fingers. And yet somehow you read that sentence twice.
winkEn.squeeze: 'A gentle squeeze' is genuine throwing technique. Context is doing all the heavy lifting here.
winkEn.pull: 'Pulling up' is the real name of a wheel technique. Please read it with a perfectly straight face.
winkEn.hotter: Kilns really do get hotter as they go. So does this conversation, but that is surely a coincidence.
winkEn.heat: Nothing gets strong without a little heat — strictly a ceramics fact. Probably.
winkEn.door: Leaving the studio door open just means staying late to welcome you. Anything more is left to your imagination.

0000.name: 느긋한 손끝
0000.en: A week later, your first little bowl comes out of the kiln, and you cradle it like something rare. You never rushed a single step, and it shows in every smooth line. Some hands, Mrs. Clay says, are simply born patient.
0000.ko: 일주일 뒤, 첫 그릇이 가마에서 나오고, 당신은 그걸 귀한 물건처럼 두 손으로 감싸 쥐어요. 어느 한 걸음도 서두르지 않았고, 그 마음이 매끈한 곡선마다 배어 있어요. 어떤 손은 그저 참을성 있게 태어난다고, Clay 선생님이 말해요.

0001.name: 마지막에 문을 두드린 손
0001.en: You were gentle and careful all evening — until the very end, when you said you might come every night. Mrs. Clay only smiled and did not say no. Now the studio light stays on a little later on the nights you visit.
0001.ko: 저녁 내내 부드럽고 조심스러웠는데, 맨 끝에 가서 매일 밤이라도 오겠다고 했죠. Clay 선생님은 그저 웃을 뿐 안 된다고는 하지 않았어요. 이제 당신이 오는 날이면 공방 불이 조금 더 늦게까지 켜져 있어요.

0010.name: 더위를 눈치챈 사람
0010.nameEn: The One Who Noticed the Heat
0010.en: Quiet hands all night, except for one small question: was it always this warm in here? Mrs. Clay laughed and blamed the kiln. She still teases you about it whenever you arrive, and somehow the studio feels a little warmer each week.
0010.ko: 밤새 손은 얌전했는데, 딱 하나 물었죠. 여기 원래 이렇게 따뜻하냐고요. Clay 선생님은 웃으며 가마 탓을 했어요. 당신이 올 때마다 아직도 그 얘기로 놀리는데, 이상하게 공방은 매주 조금씩 더 따뜻해지는 것 같아요.

0011.name: 끝이 뜨거운 수강생
0011.en: You began shy and cool, but by the end you noticed the heat and asked to come every night. Mrs. Clay likes a student who warms up slowly. "The best pots," she says, "start cold and finish hot."
0011.ko: 수줍고 서늘하게 시작했지만, 끝에 가서는 열기를 알아채고 매일 밤 오겠다고 했죠. Clay 선생님은 천천히 달아오르는 수강생을 좋아해요. "제일 좋은 도자기는 차갑게 시작해서 뜨겁게 끝나는 법이죠." 선생님이 말해요.

0100.name: 손을 맡긴 견습생
0100.en: You did everything carefully, but once, when the clay kept slipping, you asked her to steady your hands — and she did, for a long while. You never mentioned it again, yet Mrs. Clay stands a little closer at the wheel now.
0100.ko: 다 조심스럽게 했지만, 점토가 자꾸 미끄러질 때 딱 한 번 선생님께 손을 잡아 달라 했죠. 선생님은 한참 동안 그렇게 해 줬고요. 다시는 그 얘길 꺼내지 않았는데, Clay 선생님은 요즘 물레 앞에서 조금 더 가까이 서 있어요.

0101.name: 손도 마음도 맡긴 사람
0101.nameEn: The One Who Handed It All Over
0101.en: You let her steady your hands in the middle and asked to come every night at the end. In between, you were the picture of a careful student. "Funny," Mrs. Clay says, "how the quiet ones keep the key to my studio."
0101.ko: 중간엔 선생님께 손을 맡기고, 끝엔 매일 밤 오겠다고 했죠. 그 사이엔 더없이 얌전한 수강생이었고요. "묘하죠. 우리 공방 열쇠는 늘 조용한 사람들이 챙겨 가더라니까요." Clay 선생님이 말해요.

0110.name: 은근히 다 아는 손
0110.en: You asked her to steady your hands, you noticed how warm the room was, and yet you went home without asking for more. Mrs. Clay watched you leave with a puzzled smile. "All that instinct," she sighs to the kiln, "and he only comes once a week."
0110.ko: 선생님께 손을 잡아 달라 하고, 방이 얼마나 따뜻한지도 눈치챘으면서, 더 오겠다는 말 없이 집에 갔죠. Clay 선생님은 알쏭달쏭한 미소로 당신을 배웅했어요. "저런 감각을 가지고, 겨우 일주일에 한 번이라니." 선생님이 가마를 보며 한숨지어요.

0111.name: 느리게 타오른 불
0111.en: You started as a nervous beginner, then let her guide your hands, felt the heat, and booked every night you could. Mrs. Clay says beginners like you are her favorite kind. "They start out cold," she smiles, "and end up running the kiln."
0111.ko: 떨리는 초보로 시작했다가, 선생님께 손을 맡기고, 열기를 느끼고, 올 수 있는 밤은 전부 예약했죠. Clay 선생님은 당신 같은 초보가 제일 좋대요. "차갑게 시작해서, 결국엔 가마를 도맡는 사람이 되거든요." 선생님이 미소 지어요.

1000.name: 말만 앞선 손
1000.en: "My hands are ready," you announced — then spent the rest of the night doing everything slowly and carefully. Mrs. Clay found it charming. "Bold words, gentle hands," she says. "Honestly, the safest kind of student."
1000.ko: "제 손은 준비됐어요"라더니, 그 뒤로는 밤새 모든 걸 천천히, 조심스럽게 했죠. Clay 선생님은 그게 오히려 귀엽대요. "말은 과감하고 손은 부드럽고. 사실 그런 수강생이 제일 안전하죠." 선생님이 말해요.

1001.name: 약속을 남긴 사람
1001.nameEn: The One Who Left a Promise
1001.en: You opened with ready hands and closed by promising to come every night, with a whole evening of careful work in between. Mrs. Clay likes a student who keeps a promise. Now the two ends of your week are the same: her studio, your hands.
1001.ko: 준비된 손으로 열고, 매일 밤 오겠다는 약속으로 닫았죠. 그 사이엔 밤새 성실하게 작업했고요. Clay 선생님은 약속 지키는 수강생을 좋아해요. 이제 당신 한 주의 시작과 끝은 똑같아요. 선생님의 공방, 그리고 당신의 손.

1010.name: 능청스러운 물레잡이
1010.en: Ready hands at the start, a cheeky word about the heat near the end — you kept things light and never asked for more. Somehow, though, the wheel next to Mrs. Clay is always free when you arrive. Funny how that works.
1010.ko: 시작은 준비된 손, 끝 무렵엔 열기를 두고 던진 능청스러운 한마디. 가볍게 넘기고 더 바라지도 않았죠. 그런데 이상하게 당신이 오면 Clay 선생님 옆 물레는 늘 비어 있어요. 참 신기한 일이죠.

1011.name: 혼자서도 잘하는 사람
1011.en: Confident hands, a knowing word about the heat, and every night booked — you did it all, and only once, at the wheel, did you play it careful. Mrs. Clay keeps the studio open just for you now. "Fast learners," she says, "deserve extra hours."
1011.ko: 자신 있는 손, 열기를 두고 던진 한마디, 그리고 예약된 매일 밤. 다 해내고, 물레 앞에서 딱 한 번만 신중했죠. 이제 Clay 선생님은 당신만을 위해 공방을 열어 둬요. "빨리 배우는 사람은 시간을 더 받을 자격이 있죠." 선생님이 말해요.

1100.name: 손 넷의 재능꾼
1100.en: You came in bold and let her steady your hands, but you finished the night quiet and left without asking for more. Mrs. Clay still talks about your grip. "Such good hands," she tells the kiln, "and he only stayed one lesson."
1100.ko: 과감하게 들어와 선생님께 손을 맡겼는데, 밤은 조용히 마무리하고 더 바라는 것 없이 떠났죠. Clay 선생님은 아직도 당신의 손매 얘길 해요. "그렇게 좋은 손을 가지고, 딱 한 번 배우고 가다니." 선생님이 가마에게 말해요.

1101.name: 다시 오는 큰손
1101.nameEn: The Regular with the Golden Hands
1101.en: Ready hands, her hands over yours, and every night booked before she could offer — the only calm moment was one quiet question at the wheel. Mrs. Clay says four hands make the best pots. Yours, it seems, are booked solid now.
1101.ko: 준비된 손, 당신 손 위에 포개진 선생님의 손, 그리고 선생님이 권하기도 전에 잡아 둔 매일 밤. 유일하게 차분했던 건 물레 앞에서의 조용한 질문 하나뿐이었죠. Clay 선생님은 손이 넷일 때 제일 좋은 도자기가 나온다고 해요. 당신 손은 이제 예약이 꽉 찬 것 같고요.

1110.name: 다 가진 채 돌아선 사람
1110.en: Bold from the first hello, her hands over yours, the heat noticed out loud — and then, at the very end, you chose to wait for it to cool. Mrs. Clay laughed for a long time. "Even the boldest hands," she says, "know when to be patient."
1110.ko: 첫인사부터 과감했고, 선생님의 손이 당신 손을 감쌌고, 열기까지 소리 내어 짚었으면서 — 맨 끝에 가서는 식을 때까지 기다리기로 했죠. Clay 선생님은 한참을 웃었어요. "제일 과감한 손도 언제 참아야 하는지는 아는 법이죠." 선생님이 말해요.

1111.name: 가마를 지핀 사람
1111.nameEn: The One Who Stoked the Kiln
1111.en: Ready hands, her hands over yours, the heat named out loud, and every night claimed as your own — Mrs. Clay finally met a pair of hands that could keep up. By summer, the whole town knows her studio never really closes anymore. The kiln, the neighbors say, is not the only thing kept burning after dark.
1111.ko: 준비된 손, 당신 손을 감싼 선생님의 손, 소리 내어 짚은 열기, 그리고 통째로 차지한 매일 밤 — Clay 선생님은 드디어 자기 속도를 따라오는 손을 만났어요. 여름이 되자 온 동네가 알아요, 그 공방은 이제 진짜로 문 닫는 법이 없다는 걸요. 해가 진 뒤에도 계속 타오르는 게 가마만은 아니라고, 이웃들은 말해요.

introEn:
Mrs. Clay teaches the pottery wheel, hands over hands. Perfectly wholesome.
...on the surface.
